title: Werauhia rubra
taxonomy: Werauhia rubra belongs to the family Bromeliaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as bromeliads. This family includes over 3,000 species of plants, many of which are native to the Americas. Werauhia rubra is a type of epiphyte, meaning it grows on other plants or objects but does not obtain nutrients from them. The genus Werauhia is a relatively small group of plants within the Bromeliaceae family, with many species found in South America. Werauhia rubra is often used as an ornamental plant due to its striking red flowers and vibrant green foliage.

The plant's extracts have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it useful for preventing the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms. intro: Werauhia rubra, also known as the Red Bromeliad, is a species of flowering plant in the bromeliad family, characterized by its vibrant red flowers and long, narrow leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Werauhia rubra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Werauhia rubra include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and promote well-being.
